subject: error handling in render
old: errors thrown during render were caught and rethrown, and in DEV logged to console.error
new: uncaught errors are reported to window.reportError; errors caught by an Error Boundary are reported to console.error
truth: Stop relying on render errors being re-thrown; uncaught render errors now go to window.reportError instead of being re-thrown.
quote: Old: errors thrown during render were caught and rethrown, and in DEV logged to console.error, causing duplicate logs. New: uncaught errors are reported to window.reportError; errors caught by an Error Boundary are reported to console.error.

from_fact: errors thrown during render were caught and rethrown, and in dev logged to console.error
uncaught errors are reported to window.reporterror; errors caught by an error boundary are reported to console.error

subject: PropTypes on function components
truth: Stop using PropTypes on function components; they are silently ignored.
quote: PropTypes checks are removed from the React package and are silently ignored.

subject: defaultProps on function components
truth: Stop using defaultProps on function components; use ES6 default parameters instead.
quote: defaultProps is removed from function components; use ES6 default parameters instead.

subject: Legacy Context (contextTypes and getChildContext)
truth: Stop using contextTypes and getChildContext; use React.createContext() and static contextType instead.
quote: Legacy Context using contextTypes and getChildContext is removed. Use React.createContext() and static contextType instead.

subject: string refs
truth: Stop using string refs; use callback refs like ref={input => this.input = input} instead.
quote: String refs like ref='input' are removed. Use a callback ref: ref={input => this.input = input}.

subject: Module pattern factory components
old: factory components returning an object with a render method
truth: Stop using module pattern factory components that return an object with a render method; return JSX directly.
quote: Module pattern factory components (returning an object with a render method) are removed; return JSX directly.

from_fact: factory components returning an object with a render method

subject: React.createFactory
truth: Stop using React.createFactory; use JSX like <button /> instead.
quote: React.createFactory is removed; use JSX instead, e.g. <button />.

subject: react-test-renderer/shallow
truth: Stop using react-test-renderer/shallow; install react-shallow-renderer and import ShallowRenderer from it.
quote: react-test-renderer/shallow is removed; install react-shallow-renderer and import ShallowRenderer from react-shallow-renderer.

subject: act from react-dom/test-utils
truth: Stop importing act from react-dom/test-utils; import act from react instead.
quote: act is removed from react-dom/test-utils; import act from react instead.

subject: all other react-dom/test-utils functions
old: all other test-utils functions besides act
truth: Stop using any react-dom/test-utils functions; they have all been removed.
quote: All other test-utils functions have been removed.

from_fact: all other test-utils functions besides act

subject: ReactDOM.render
truth: Stop using ReactDOM.render; use createRoot from react-dom/client: const root = createRoot(container); root.render(<App />).
quote: ReactDOM.render is removed. Use createRoot from react-dom/client: const root = createRoot(container); root.render(<App />).

subject: ReactDOM.hydrate
truth: Stop using ReactDOM.hydrate; use hydrateRoot from react-dom/client: hydrateRoot(container, <App />).
quote: ReactDOM.hydrate is removed. Use hydrateRoot from react-dom/client: hydrateRoot(container, <App />).

subject: unmountComponentAtNode
truth: Stop using unmountComponentAtNode; use root.unmount() instead.
quote: unmountComponentAtNode is removed; use root.unmount() instead.

subject: ReactDOM.findDOMNode
truth: Stop using ReactDOM.findDOMNode; use refs (useRef) instead.
quote: ReactDOM.findDOMNode is removed; use refs (useRef) instead.

subject: element.ref access
truth: Stop accessing element.ref; use element.props.ref instead, as ref is now a regular prop.
quote: Accessing element.ref is deprecated in favor of element.props.ref; ref is now a regular prop.

subject: react-test-renderer
truth: Stop using react-test-renderer; use @testing-library/react instead.
quote: react-test-renderer is deprecated in favor of @testing-library/react; it logs a deprecation warning and switched to concurrent rendering in React 19.

subject: ref callback return value in TypeScript
old: ref callback with implicit return like ref={current => (instance = current)}
truth: Stop using implicit return in ref callbacks; use a block body like ref={current => {instance = current}} or TypeScript will reject it.
quote: A ref callback with an implicit return like ref={current => (instance = current)} is now rejected by TypeScript; use a block body: ref={current => {instance = current}}.

from_fact: ref callback with implicit return like ref={current => (instance = current)}

subject: useRef() without argument in TypeScript
truth: Stop calling useRef() without an argument; call useRef(undefined) as it now requires an argument.
quote: useRef() now requires an argument; call useRef(undefined).

subject: ReactElement props type in TypeScript
truth: Stop assuming ReactElement props are any; they now default to unknown, requiring type narrowing.
quote: The props of React elements now default to unknown instead of any.

subject: global JSX namespace in TypeScript
truth: Stop using the global JSX namespace; use React.JSX and wrap module augmentation inside declare module 'react'.
quote: The global JSX namespace is replaced; use React.JSX and wrap module augmentation inside declare module "react".

subject: unstable_flushControlled from react-dom
truth: Stop using unstable_flushControlled from react-dom; it has been removed.
quote: react-dom removes unstable_flushControlled, unstable_createEventHandle, unstable_renderSubtreeIntoContainer, and unstable_runWithPriority.

subject: unstable_createEventHandle from react-dom
truth: Stop using unstable_createEventHandle from react-dom; it has been removed.
quote: react-dom removes unstable_flushControlled, unstable_createEventHandle, unstable_renderSubtreeIntoContainer, and unstable_runWithPriority.

subject: unstable_renderSubtreeIntoContainer from react-dom
truth: Stop using unstable_renderSubtreeIntoContainer from react-dom; it has been removed.
quote: react-dom removes unstable_flushControlled, unstable_createEventHandle, unstable_renderSubtreeIntoContainer, and unstable_runWithPriority.

subject: unstable_runWithPriority from react-dom
truth: Stop using unstable_runWithPriority from react-dom; it has been removed.
quote: react-dom removes unstable_flushControlled, unstable_createEventHandle, unstable_renderSubtreeIntoContainer, and unstable_runWithPriority.

subject: errorInfo.digest from onRecoverableError
truth: Stop accessing errorInfo.digest in onRecoverableError; it has been removed.
quote: errorInfo.digest is removed from onRecoverableError.

subject: JavaScript URLs in src and href attributes
old: JavaScript URLs like javascript: were allowed in src and href
new: JavaScript URLs in src and href now error
truth: Stop using javascript: URLs in src and href attributes; they now throw an error.
quote: JavaScript URLs in src and href now error.

from_fact: javascript urls like javascript: were allowed in src and href
javascript urls in src and href now error

subject: UMD builds
truth: Stop using UMD builds of React; use an ESM-based CDN like esm.sh instead.
quote: UMD builds are no longer produced; use an ESM-based CDN such as esm.sh.

subject: JSX transform
truth: Stop using the old JSX transform; the new JSX transform (2020) is now required because React 19 features like ref as a prop depend on it.
quote: The new JSX transform (2020) is now required; React 19 features like 'ref' as a prop require it. A warning is shown if the outdated transform is used.

subject: ref callback invocation in StrictMode
old: Ref callbacks were invoked once on initial mount in development StrictMode
new: Ref callbacks are double-invoked on initial mount in development StrictMode
truth: In development StrictMode, ref callbacks are now double-invoked on initial mount, which may cause issues if your ref callback has side effects or assumes single invocation.
quote: Ref callbacks are double-invoked on initial mount.

from_fact: ref callbacks were invoked once on initial mount in development strictmode
ref callbacks are double-invoked on initial mount in development strictmode
